summary: A solid late‑80s audience recording that captures the crowd vibe with only a hint of high‑end fuzz, highlighted by a great Playing in the Band → Uncle John's Band segue and a nicely rendered All Along The Watchtower.
review: This tape is an excellent example of what a well‑tapered audience source can deliver from the late‑80s era—clear enough to hear arena nuances with just a tiny high‑frequency fuzz. Musically the band warms up slowly but still provides a solid set, featuring a standout PITB > U.J. transition and an inspired All Along The Watchtower, while the remainder of the performance is fairly average for the period.

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed review gives a positive (4‑star) rating; the show is described as solid with a few highlights, and the audience recording is noted as good despite minor high‑end fuzz.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear arena sound with nuanced crowd ambience; only a very small amount of high‑end "fuzz" audible on headphones.

standout_songs: Playing in the Band > Uncle John's Band
show_quality.setlist_flow: The band appears to warm up slowly early in the run, delivering a solid but not inspired first set and picking up momentum with highlights like the PITB > U.J. segue; overall pacing is steady though the rest of the set is described as average 1988 fare.
